title: Palpitations and Dizziness - Atrial Fibrillation
chief_complaint: Palpitations and dizziness for 3 days, occasional chest discomfort

hidden_data.ecg: Absent P waves, irregularly irregular rhythm, ventricular rate ~130 bpm
hidden_data.echo: Normal LV function, left atrial enlargement, no valvular abnormalities

correct_diagnosis: Atrial Fibrillation with Rapid Ventricular Response

patient_responses.palpitations_start: 3 days ago, it just started suddenly
patient_responses.caffeine: I drink a lot of coffee

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed, Doctor. Atrial fibrillation with RVR is exactly right. You recognized the classic ECG pattern.
feedback.incorrect_message: This ECG shows atrial fibrillation with RVR. Look at the absent P waves and irregularly irregular rhythm.
feedback.partially_correct_message: You recognized the tachyarrhythmia, but the specific diagnosis is atrial fibrillation with RVR.

ecg.comment: Essential for diagnosis. You identified the rhythm correctly.

tsh.comment: Excellent. Thyroid dysfunction is a common reversible cause of AF.

echo.comment: Important to assess for structural heart disease and guide management.

cbc.comment: Reasonable to check for anemia, though not critical for diagnosis.

feedback.attending_note: Good case, Doctor. Now that you've diagnosed AF with RVR, the next step is determining rate vs rhythm control. Since she's stable, start with rate control using a beta-blocker or calcium channel blocker. Don't forget to calculate her CHA₂DS₂-VASc score for stroke risk.
